items.title: South Korean SMR-powered container ship design certified
items.summary: The Korea Research Institute of Ships & Ocean Engineering has received Approval in Principle from the American Bureau of Shipping for the conceptual design of a container ship propelled by two small modular molten salt reactors.

items.title: Pacific Fusion Says Pulsed-Power Prototype Hits Milestone at National Lab
items.summary: California-headquartered Pacific Fusion announced that a pulsed-power prototype designed and built at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory (LLNL) has surpassed 3,000 shots under a Cooperative Research and Development Agreement with the company. Pacific Fusion on July 16 said this marks a key miles

items.title: Realta Fusion, Inertia Enterprises select HQ locations
items.summary: Two nuclear fusion companies are setting up shop in disused industrial buildings. Realta Fusion just named a Madison, Wis., location for its “Realta Forge.” Meanwhile, last week, Inertia Enterprises announced the opening of its new headquarters in Livermore, Calif.

items.title: NEA irradiation system ready to deploy at MITR
items.summary: A new irradiation experimental system is ready for deployment. The rig, which is the focus of In-Core Real-Time Mechanical Testing of Structural Materials (INCREASE-I), an OECD Nuclear Energy Agency project, will be used to conduct stress-relaxation tests of stainless steel at the Massachusetts Inst

items.title: Siting licence granted for new Kola nuclear power plant units
items.summary: Russia's nuclear regulatory Rostekhnadzor has issued site licences for the first two proposed units at the new Kola Nuclear Power Plant.

items.title: Gov. Sherrill signs bill to begin nuclear procurement in N.J.
items.summary: On July 13, New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill signed the Power NJ Act, a bill that directs the state’s Board of Public Utilities (BPU), in collaboration with the state’s Economic Development Authority, to establish an “advanced nuclear energy procurement program.”

items.title: House subcommittee OKs six nuclear permitting reform bills
items.summary: The U.S. House Energy and Commerce Committee’s Subcommittee on Energy introduced six nuclear permitting reform bills back in June. The mostly bipartisan bills returned to the subcommittee on Tuesday for markups—and with strong backing from both sides of the aisle. Despite some lingering concerns in

items.title: Researchers demonstrate remote real-time adjustment of PUR-1 reactor
items.summary: The remote, automatic, real-time adjustment of a research reactor’s power by a geographically distributed control system has been reported by researchers from Idaho National Laboratory, the Grainger College of Engineering at the University of Illinois–Urbana-Champaign, and Purdue University. IAEA Draws Safeguards Conclusions for 179 States – IAEA Report
ICRS15-RPSD2026 on the horizon
items.summary: Now is a good time to register for the 15th International Conference on Radiation Shielding and the 23rd American Nuclear Society Topical Meeting of the Radiation Protection and Shielding Division (ICRS15-RPSD2026), which will be held October 25–29.

items.title: Nuclear Science is Transforming Rainbow Trout Farming in Latin America
Team brings quantum computing into FLiBe chemistry calculations
items.summary: Researchers have reported using a combination of quantum and classical computing to calculate chemistry connected to tritium speciation in a FLiBe—lithium fluoride and beryllium fluoride—molten salt blanket, demonstrating a promising direction for unlocking tritium breeding in fusion machines.

items.title: ANS webinar looks to Utah’s nuclear future
items.summary: On July 9, the American Nuclear Society hosted the third installment of its “Nuclear in the States” webinar series. This time, the webinar looked to the current landscape of Utah’s nuclear sector—from its policy developments to ongoing projects to workforce needs.ANS CEO Craig Piercy, who led the di

Terms finalized for Australian uranium exports to India
items.summary: Following up on an agreement that was signed 12 years ago, Australia and India have finalized the details by which Australian uranium will be exported to India for peaceful purposes and under the safeguards of the International Atomic Energy Agency. The Administrative Arrangement to enable the long-

items.title: Reactor vessel installed at Lufeng unit 1
items.summary: The reactor pressure vessel has been installed at unit 1 of the Lufeng nuclear power plant in China's Guangdong province, China General Nuclear announced.

items.title: NRC approves Westinghouse exemption request for AP1000 DC
items.summary: Westinghouse has cleared a hurdle in its quest to renew and update the standard design certification (DC) for its AP1000 reactor with the Nuclear Regulatory Commission’s approval of an exemption from scheduling requirements that limit when an applicant can apply for a DC renewal.

items.title: A closer look at NRC’s proposed rule eliminating ALARA
items.summary: On July 1, the Nuclear Regulatory Commission proposed removing the “as low as reasonably achievable” (ALARA) principle from its radiation protection regulations and replacing it with a graded approach. The agency also proposed increases to effluent dose limits and adjusting how it handles allowances

items.title: NRC issues white safety finding to V.C. Summer
items.summary: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission has upheld and finalized a “white” safety finding at V.C. Summer power plant in Jenkinsville, S.C., over the plant’s failure to properly preplan and perform maintenance on its turbine-driven emergency feedwater pump (TDEFW) governor valve linkage. The 966-MWe three-

items.title: Sizewell B gets 20-year life extension; SMR agreements signed around the world
items.summary: Sizewell B, on the Suffolk coast of England, has been granted a 20-year life extension. The nuclear power plant, which began operating in 1995, will now remain in operation until 2055. Sizewell B owner and operator EDF Energy agreed to make extra investments to maintain the facility, with additional

items.title: Building new Swiss nuclear power plant economically viable, report says
items.summary: The construction of a new nuclear power plant in Switzerland would be economically viable, according to a study carried out by the BAK Economics research institute on behalf of the Swiss business federation Economiesuisse.

items.title: Third reactor at Changjiang plant starts up
items.summary: Unit 3 at the Changjiang nuclear power plant in China's Hainan province has attained a sustained chain reaction for the first time, China National Nuclear Corporation has announced.

items.title: IAEA Concludes International Physical Protection Advisory Follow-Up Mission in China
NRC exempts Westinghouse from design certification renewal rule
items.summary: The US Nuclear Regulatory Commission has said Westinghouse can apply to renew the AP1000 standard design certification, incorporating lessons learned from the construction and operation of Vogtle units 3 and 4, well ahead of the expiration of the current certificate.

items.title: IAEA-Led Mission Sample ALPS-Treated Water at Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Station under Add…
First commercial nuclear-powered satellite launched
items.summary: Miami-based City Labs Inc announced that its Betavoltaic Orbital High-Reliability satellite - the world's first commercial nuclear-powered satellite and first nuclear CubeSat - was on board SpaceX Transporter-17, a rideshare mission launched on Tuesday.

items.title: IAEA Launches First Regional Radiation Protection Training Course for Caribbean States
items.summary: The IAEA has launched its first Postgraduate Educational Course (PGEC) on Radiation Protection and the Safety of Radiation Sources for Caribbean countries, marking an important milestone in strengthening radiation safety capacity across the region.

items.title: Argentina announces privately-financed SMR plan
items.summary: Argentina's government has said US-based Meitner Energy is planning to invest USD1.2 billion in the construction of a 300 MW small modular reactor at the Atucha site.

items.title: Reactor vessel installed in El Dabaa's second unit
items.summary: A ceremony has been held to mark the installation of the reactor pressure vessel in the second unit at Egypt's El Dabaa Nuclear Power Plant.

items.title: Argentina Plans 300 MW SMR at Atucha
items.summary: Argentina Plans 300 MW SMR at Atucha (NucNet contributed to this report)  US-based energy firm Meitner Energy has presented plans to the Argentine government to build a 300 MW small modular reactor (SMR) at the Atucha nuclear power station site … Continue reading →

items.title: Trilateral cooperation agreement on SMR deployment
items.summary: The USA, Japan and South Korea have signed a memorandum of cooperation to support trilateral cooperation on accelerating small modular reactor deployments in third countries, with an initial focus on the Indo-Pacific region.

items.title: Four Micro Reactors Hit Criticality Milestones Crossing DOE’s Finish Line on July 4th
items.summary: Four Micro Reactors Meet Criticality Milestones On 07/04/26 as part of the U.S. Department of Energy Reactor Pilot Program, Aalo Atomics’ advanced reactor design, Aalo-X, was the fourth firm to successfully complete a zero-power fueled criticality demonstration. Earlier in June, … Continue rea

items.title: IAEA Helps Latin American and the Caribbean Countries Prepare for Nuclear and Radiological Emer…
items.summary: The IAEA helps countries prepare for nuclear and radiological emergencies through its safety standards and guidance, as well as capacity-building initiatives.

items.title: Deep Fission receives prototype reactor canister
items.summary: California-based startup Deep Fission, which aims to place small modular reactors in boreholes a mile underground, has announced the delivery of its prototype reactor canister for its pilot project to the Parsons site in Kansas.

items.title: New facility in Denmark will let engineers test robots in handling key fusion components
items.summary: Earlier this summer, the University of Southern Denmark opened a new large-scale robotics centre in Odense. This is where EUROfusion will address one of the main unresolved engineering challenges on the path to commercial fusion power: using robots to remove radioactive components from a fusion re

items.title: IAEA and World Bank Group Collaborate to Advance Nuclear Energy for Development
Proxima Funding Round Targets 2030s for Fusion Power
items.summary: Proxima Fusion Funding Fuels 2030s Date for Power Plant German startup Proxima Fusion has raised €411 million ($469 million) in a new funding round from a range of investors, including national energy firm RWE AG and Alphabet Inc.’s Google, to … Continue reading →

items.title: Republic of Cyprus Signs a Country Programme Framework (CPF) for 2026-2031
items.summary: HE Mr Andreas Ignatiou, Resident Representative, Permanent Mission of the Republic of Cyprus to the IAEA, and Mr Hua Liu, IAEA Deputy Director General and Head of the Department of Technical Cooperation, signed the Republic of Cyprus Country Programme Framework (CPF) for the period

items.title: Criticality for fourth US microreactor to meet 4 July deadline
items.summary: Aalo Atomics announced that its Critical Test Reactor at the Idaho National Laboratory successfully achieved initial criticality in the early hours of Saturday morning, becoming the fourth microreactor under US Department of Energy authorisation to achieve the milestone by 4 July 2026.

items.title: Agreement could see prototype microreactor built at Berkeley
items.summary: Chiltern Vital Group has signed a letter of intent with Cambridge Atomworks to consider the construction of the prototype Odin microreactor on the Berkeley Green Science and Technology Park in Gloucestershire, England.

items.title: Control room simulator launched for lead-cooled BREST-OD-300 reactor
items.summary: A full-scale simulator of the control room for the lead-cooled fast neutron BREST-OD-300 reactor has been commissioned, allowing training for staff before the unit's future physical startup.

items.title: Ampera says it has 3D-printed microreactor module
items.summary: US-based Ampera Inc says it has achieved what it calls a major milestone by completing the production of the first full-scale, 3D-printed nuclear reactor module.

items.title: IAEA Launches Project on Small and Medium Sized Magnetic Devices for Fusion Research
items.summary: As fusion research expands across the public and private sectors and more countries seek to develop or join fusion research programmes, maintaining a diverse and globally connected experimental ecosystem is becoming increasingly important.

items.title: First IAEA Training on Public Communication in Nuclear Security
Nuclear Liability: Ensuring Legal Certainty and Compensation for Nuclear Damage
How the IAEA Connects Scientists to Ion-Beam Facilities
items.summary: Particle accelerators are powerful tools that drive innovation across health, agriculture, environmental studies, energy, materials science, cultural heritage and forensics.

items.title: Safety and Security of Radioactive Sources: Commitment in Action
Second Taipingling unit begins supplying power
items.summary: Unit 2 of the Taipingling nuclear power plant has begun supplying electricity to the grid for the first time, China General Nuclear announced. The unit is the second of six Hualong One (HPR1000) reactors planned for the site in Guangdong province.
